name: Century Bank And Trust
description_short: FDIC-insured regional bank founded in 1898 offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and wealth management services with emphasis on relationship banking.
description_long:
Century Bank & Trust has operated since 1898, positioning itself as an established community bank focused on relationship-based financial services. The bank recently announced the acquisition of Bank of Wrightsville, indicating ongoing growth and consolidation activity in their market. They operate as a full-service financial institution with FDIC insurance backing, providing consumers and businesses with traditional banking products and competitive financial solutions. The company emphasizes local decision-making and experienced staff as core differentiators in an increasingly digital banking landscape.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking products including multiple checking account tiers (Century Cash Checking, Century Cash Back Checking, NOW Personal Checking, Regular Checking), savings vehicles (regular savings, money market accounts, Christmas savings accounts), certificates of deposit, and individual retirement accounts. For borrowing, they provide personal loans, home mortgage loans, home equity loans, auto loans, and personal term loans. Business customers can access analyzed business checking, business savings, business money market accounts, business CDs, business retirement accounts, business term loans, lines of credit, construction loans, and commercial real estate loans. Additional services include credit cards for both personal and business use, wealth management, estate planning, investment management, trust services, digital banking, debit cards, and merchant services.

Century Bank & Trust distinguishes itself through its emphasis on local, relationship-based banking and hometown service. Multiple customer testimonials highlight the personal touch—customers mention being greeted by name, feeling like family, and appreciating the combination of professional operations with a hometown feel. The bank specifically markets its experienced loan officers and mortgage team available for consultation. Their 125+ year operational history and recent acquisition of another bank suggest financial stability and market presence. The website emphasizes accessibility features and provides comprehensive financial calculators and resources for consumers.

Limitations include their apparent geographic concentration (references to Wrightsville and Middle Georgia suggest regional rather than national footprint), lack of transparency on their website regarding specific rates, terms, or APR ranges for loans and credit products, and no evidence of specialized products for credit-challenged consumers. The website content focuses heavily on relationship banking benefits but provides minimal quantitative information about competitiveness on rates or fees. For consumers seeking specific rate comparisons or those outside their service area, this bank may not be the optimal choice.
services: Personal checking accounts (multiple tiers including rewards)
Personal savings accounts, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit
Personal loans and personal term loans
Home mortgage loans and home equity loans
Business checking accounts (regular, analyzed, and NOW checking)
Business savings, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit
Business term loans and lines of credit
Construction loans and commercial real estate loans
Wealth management, estate planning, investment management, and trust services
Merchant card services and cash management
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Founded in 1898 with 125+ years of operational history and stability
Multiple checking account options including cash back rewards (Century Cash Back Checking)
Comprehensive loan portfolio including personal, auto, mortgage, home equity, business, construction, and commercial real estate loans
Dedicated loan officers and mortgage team available for local, in-person consultation and decision-making
Wealth management services including estate planning, investment management, and trust services
Mobile banking app available for iOS allowing bill pay, transaction review, and account access
cons: No specific rate, APR, or fee information displayed on website—consumers cannot compare competitiveness without contacting bank directly
Appears to be geographically limited regional bank with no indication of national reach or online-only account opening
No evidence of products or services for credit-challenged consumers or credit repair
Website does not clearly explain differences between similar account types (e.g., Century Cash vs. Century Cash Back Checking)
Limited digital innovation marketing—emphasis on relationship banking may indicate slower digital-first service adoption compared to neobanks or national competitors
best_for: Small business owners and entrepreneurs in their service area seeking relationship-based business banking with local decision-makers
Consumers prioritizing in-person service and personal relationships over digital-first banking
Borrowers seeking mortgages, construction loans, or commercial real estate financing with experienced local loan officers
diagnosis: Century Bank & Trust is best for small to mid-sized business owners and personal customers in their regional service area who value relationship banking, local decision-making, and in-person service over digital convenience and rate transparency. The primary caveat is lack of publicly available rate and fee information on their website, requiring direct contact for competitive comparison, and their apparent regional geographic limitation may exclude consumers outside their service territory.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
